jokes.text: Thompson's Teeth... the only teeth strong enough to eat other teeth
jokes.setup: Fake commercial for dental product
jokes.punchline: Teeth that eat other teeth - cannibalistic dental care
jokes.explanation: Classic Futurama absurdist fake commercial. Dark humor about cannibalistic teeth is original and memorable.

jokes.text: Dear Employee: Has it really been 5, 10 or 15 years? If not, disregard this and get to work. Distribute token of appreciation and applaud.
jokes.setup: Reading a mandatory anniversary speech
jokes.punchline: Corporate form letter that doesn't even apply to the employee
jokes.explanation: Good satire of corporate insincerity and form letters. Well-structured with clear setup/payoff.

jokes.text: Look! Coupons! I can get two oil changes for the price of one! Now if I could only afford the one. And the car.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg is excited about oil change coupons
jokes.punchline: He can't afford oil changes or even a car
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg character moment - poverty escalation with good structure and timing.

jokes.text: Yes, yes. Now here's your pension statement. It's empty because you haven't paid into it, you dummy.
jokes.setup: Formal presentation of pension statement
jokes.punchline: Casually calling employee a dummy for not paying in
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Farnsworth's blunt honesty, but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: I got plans. I'm gonna turn my on/off switch to off.
jokes.setup: Discussion about retirement planning
jokes.punchline: Robot suicide as retirement plan
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that's perfectly robot-specific. Good character integration with Bender's nihilism.

jokes.text: Also, I need athlete's foot cream for my face.
jokes.setup: Fry checking his meager retirement fund
jokes.punchline: Non-sequitur about facial athlete's foot cream
jokes.explanation: Random absurd line that fits Fry's character but lacks clear setup/structure.

jokes.text: Cherry. Cherry! Mule. Crud!
jokes.setup: Fry playing lottery machine
jokes.punchline: Getting close but losing on a mule symbol
jokes.explanation: Standard gambling near-miss gag. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: I've seen lines move faster in the sperm bank.
jokes.setup: Complaining about slow bank line
jokes.punchline: Comparing to sperm bank lines
jokes.explanation: Good unexpected comparison joke with sexual edge typical of Bender.

jokes.text: You old lunatic! How you been? / Not bad. Not bad.
jokes.setup: Friendly reunion greeting
jokes.punchline: Casual conversation between criminal robot friends
jokes.explanation: Sets up casual criminal friendship dynamic - serviceable setup more than standalone joke

jokes.text: I'm okay too. I'm taking a Chinese cooking class at the Learning Annex.
jokes.setup: Roberto is robbing the bank
jokes.punchline: Bender casually continues conversation during robbery
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Bender's obliviousness to danger while making small talk during a robbery. Perfect timing.

jokes.text: My caddie-chauffeur informs me that a bank is where people put money that isn't invested right.
jokes.setup: Judge explaining bank robbery charges
jokes.punchline: Rich judge doesn't understand banks
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment for out-of-touch wealthy judge. Shows class satire.

jokes.text: Your Honor, I move that I be disbarred for introducing this evidence against my own clients.
jokes.setup: Lawyer introducing damning evidence
jokes.punchline: Asking to be disbarred for being terrible at his job
jokes.explanation: Clever legal satire - lawyer acknowledging his incompetence in formal legal language.

jokes.text: Just give a name! You don't wanna look stupid on Court TV.
jokes.setup: Fry struggling to remember Roberto's name
jokes.punchline: Lawyer more concerned about TV appearance than justice
jokes.explanation: Decent media satire but fairly obvious observation about reality TV culture.

jokes.text: And the other hamburger will also be made of your lungs. So long, pal.
jokes.setup: Roberto threatening Fry on the phone
jokes.punchline: Specific threat about lung hamburgers
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Roberto - creative and disturbing threat delivered casually. Good character voice.

jokes.text: I won't testify on grounds that my organs will be chopped up into a patty. The 67th Amendment.
jokes.setup: Fry refusing to testify
jokes.punchline: Made-up constitutional amendment about organ patties
jokes.explanation: Creative legal absurdity. Good concept but could be tighter in execution.

jokes.text: I may be a simple, country chicken, but I know when we're finger-licked.
jokes.setup: Lawyer assessing their legal situation
jokes.punchline: KFC pun with chicken/finger-licked
jokes.explanation: Solid pun that works on multiple levels. Well-constructed but reliant on brand recognition.

jokes.text: They done hired me to represent them. Insanity plea is accepted.
jokes.setup: What evidence supports insanity plea?
jokes.punchline: Hiring this lawyer IS evidence of insanity
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-deprecating lawyer joke with perfect setup/payoff structure.

jokes.text: Yahoo! The system fails again!
jokes.setup: Being sentenced to asylum
jokes.punchline: Bender celebrates legal system dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - celebrates corruption that benefits him

jokes.text: The only poor people I want to hear about are those who tend to my pores at the spa.
jokes.setup: Judge ordering no talk about poor people
jokes.punchline: Poor/pores pun about spa workers
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing judge's elitism, with workable pun structure.

jokes.text: Ow, my head! Ow, my feet! Ow, my head! Ow, my feet!
jokes.setup: Fry being transported to asylum
jokes.punchline: Alternating head and feet pain
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy bit. Functional but not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: Well, you meet half the qualifications.
jokes.setup: Fry complaining this is for insane robots
jokes.punchline: Implying Fry is insane
jokes.explanation: Good Bender insult with clean logic. Economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: What the...? I find that offensive!
jokes.setup: Fry being examined by robot medical equipment
jokes.punchline: Reaction to inappropriate probing
jokes.explanation: Standard inappropriate touching gag. Relies on implied visual more than clever writing.

jokes.text: [Visual gag: Medical examination reveals Fry as robot on scanner]
jokes.setup: Fry confident physical exam will prove he's human
jokes.punchline: Scanner shows robot anatomy despite his obvious humanity
jokes.explanation: Strong visual subversion of expectations - technology failing creates absurd situation

jokes.text: See? I'm all squishy and flabby. Also, I complain a lot.
jokes.setup: Fry trying to prove he's human
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating list of human flaws
jokes.explanation: Good character self-awareness. The complaining line is particularly fitting for Fry.

jokes.text: The car dealer? I guess his prices really were insane.
jokes.setup: Meeting Malfunctioning Eddie in asylum
jokes.punchline: Reference to his 'insane prices' slogan
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to established character. Works on multiple levels for fans.

jokes.text: We've met. What?!
jokes.setup: Polite introduction to roommate
jokes.punchline: Eddie explodes at mild surprise
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for Eddie's malfunction. The mundane trigger makes it funnier.

jokes.text: [Malfunctioning Eddie explodes from surprise]
jokes.setup: Warning not to surprise the excitable robot
jokes.punchline: He literally explodes from being told they've met before
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff of setup warning with character-specific physical comedy

jokes.text: They don't believe I'm a human either. Name's Unit 2013.
jokes.setup: Fry complaining about being mistaken for robot
jokes.punchline: Another 'human' with a robot serial number name
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reveal. The name immediately undercuts his claim to humanity.

jokes.text: Still picking up CIA transmissions on your teeth? They just won't stop! The CIA cafeteria menu for the week of May 15th is as follows: Monday, shepherd's pie...
jokes.setup: Introduction to paranoid patient
jokes.punchline: Specific, mundane CIA intelligence about cafeteria menu
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of conspiracy paranoia with banal cafeteria details. Creative take on the trope.

jokes.text: Problem is, he's got multiple personalities. All of them Lincoln.
jokes.setup: Explaining patient who thinks he's Lincoln
jokes.punchline: Multiple personality disorder but all the same person
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of multiple personality trope. Perfectly economical with great absurdist logic.

jokes.text: I was born in 200 log cabins.
jokes.setup: Patient with multiple Lincoln personalities
jokes.punchline: Multiplying Lincoln's humble origins
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up to the multiple Lincoln setup. Mathematically absurd historical reference.

jokes.text: How's working the lunchroom? - All right. - Poor Frankie.
jokes.setup: Frankie thinks he's a lunchroom worker so they put him there
jokes.punchline: He's actually working the lunchroom but they pity him for it
jokes.explanation: Clever institutional irony - his 'delusion' became his actual job but he's still pitied

jokes.text: Unless they're Oreos, and only in the mouth.
jokes.setup: Fry complaining about disks being crammed into him
jokes.punchline: Exception for Oreos in mouth only
jokes.explanation: Good Fry character moment - food obsession even in crisis. Clear structure with reasonable payoff.

jokes.text: Give me! Give me!
jokes.setup: Fry would have starved without vending machine
jokes.punchline: Vending machine begging to give away food
jokes.explanation: Clever reversal of normal vending machine interaction. Shows robot's mental illness well.

jokes.text: Lighten up, honey. I'm getting through a difficult time using humor.
jokes.setup: Leela criticizing Bender for not helping Fry
jokes.punchline: Bender claims his callousness is therapeutic coping
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender rationalization - turns his selfishness into self-care psychology

jokes.text: Thirty days, September, April, June. Peanut butter.
jokes.setup: Fry's mental state deteriorating
jokes.punchline: Mixing nursery rhyme with random food item
jokes.explanation: Shows Fry's mental deterioration effectively but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: That first time was just to case the joint and rob it a little.
jokes.setup: Asking why he robbed the same bank twice
jokes.punchline: First robbery was just reconnaissance and casual theft
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roberto logic - treating bank robbery casually. Great character consistency and absurdist reasoning.

jokes.text: That first time was just to case the joint and rob it a little.
jokes.setup: Roberto explaining why he robbed same bank twice
jokes.punchline: Claims first robbery was just reconnaissance and partial theft
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roberto logic - treats major crime as casual preparation for bigger crime

jokes.text: Now stand back. I gotta practice my stabbing.
jokes.setup: Roberto talking to his new roommate Fry
jokes.punchline: Casual announcement of violent practice session
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Roberto - treating violence as mundane hobby. Economical and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Quit cowering. You call yourself a robot?
jokes.setup: Fry cowering from Roberto's stabbing practice
jokes.punchline: Roberto thinks Fry is robot and should be braver
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Roberto questioning Fry's robot credentials. Shows everyone believes Fry is a robot now.

jokes.text: Affirmative. I feel nothing. I am a robot. Beep, beep, beep...
jokes.setup: Doctor asking if Fry is cured of humanity delusions
jokes.punchline: Fry now fully believes he's a robot
jokes.explanation: Effective character development showing Fry's complete transformation. The beeping is appropriately robotic.

jokes.text: I think I'm a doctor, but that doesn't make me a doctor. These clothes do.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg explaining thinking doesn't make reality
jokes.punchline: Credits his medical authority entirely to his outfit
jokes.explanation: Excellent Zoidberg logic that accidentally reveals the arbitrary nature of professional authority

jokes.text: I must be a robot. Why else would human women not date me?
jokes.setup: Fry explaining why he thinks he's a robot
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating dating logic
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about Fry's romantic failures. Logical within his delusional state.

jokes.text: I must be a robot. Why else would human women not date me?
jokes.setup: Fry's robotic reasoning about his identity
jokes.punchline: Uses his dating failures as evidence he's not human
jokes.explanation: Perfect blend of Fry's dating incompetence with his robotic brainwashing

jokes.text: Oh, lots of reasons.
jokes.setup: Fry's rhetorical question about dating
jokes.punchline: Amy immediately listing many non-robot reasons
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response. Economical and brutal. Amy's matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Oh, lots of reasons.
jokes.setup: Fry asking why women won't date him
jokes.punchline: Leela's blunt confirmation of his many flaws
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leela deadpan delivery with maximum comedic economy

jokes.text: Remember, I didn't squeal? Do you?
jokes.setup: Bender surprised to see Roberto in asylum
jokes.punchline: Desperately reminds Roberto he didn't testify against him
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Bender's cowardly self-preservation instincts

jokes.text: Relax, mammal. My robotic software shall meet your calculatory needs.
jokes.setup: Fry offering to help with math
jokes.punchline: Overly formal robot speech with made-up word 'calculatory'
jokes.explanation: Good robot voice parody. 'Calculatory' is a nice made-up technical term.

jokes.text: Fear not, for I shall assist ye! / Robots don't say 'ye.'
jokes.setup: Fry offering help in archaic speech
jokes.punchline: Hermes pointing out speech pattern inconsistency
jokes.explanation: Good observation about Fry's confused robot persona - logical critique of his act

jokes.text: That's a plus sign, you loony! Quit thinking you're a robot!
jokes.setup: Fry asking what a basic math symbol is
jokes.punchline: Hermes exasperated by Fry's robot delusion affecting basic knowledge
jokes.explanation: Shows how Fry's delusion is affecting his competence. Good character frustration moment.

jokes.text: I'll show ye.
jokes.setup: Fry insisting he's a robot
jokes.punchline: Using archaic 'ye' while trying to sound robotic
jokes.explanation: Mild character confusion between different speech patterns. Not particularly strong or memorable.

jokes.text: You're going to do his laundry?
jokes.setup: Leela saying she'll remind Fry of his humanity 'the way only a woman can'
jokes.punchline: Amy assumes this means domestic chores
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of romantic cliché. Amy's practical interpretation is funnier than expected.

jokes.text: By a scallop's forelocks, what's with all the beer?
jokes.setup: Fry questioning alcohol consumption
jokes.punchline: Uses nautical/sea creature oath while acting robotic
jokes.explanation: Quirky oath shows Fry's humanity leaking through robot programming

jokes.text: Alcohol fuels my power cells. And, as a mighty robot, I... Beep.
jokes.setup: Fry explaining why he drinks beer as a robot
jokes.punchline: Trailing off mid-sentence into simple beep
jokes.explanation: Shows Fry's robot performance breaking down. The anticlimatic beep is well-timed.

jokes.text: Wow. Hitting the same place three times? I admire your style.
jokes.setup: Roberto robbing same bank third time
jokes.punchline: Bank employee expresses professional admiration for repeat crime
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist reaction - victim appreciating criminal's persistence

jokes.text: He looks like a little insane drunken angel.
jokes.setup: Fry passed out and drooling
jokes.punchline: Romantic description of clearly unromantic sight
jokes.explanation: Good Leela character moment - her ability to romanticize Fry's flaws. Nice contradictory imagery.

jokes.text: Do you have any better hostages?
jokes.setup: Roberto taking hostages
jokes.punchline: Police asking for higher quality hostages
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of hostage situation trope. Police treating it like a customer service issue.

jokes.text: Fifty-six-ish?
jokes.setup: Roberto asking to guess number between 1-10
jokes.punchline: Fry guessing number way outside the range with '-ish'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry stupidity - completely missing simple instructions. The '-ish' makes it even more absurd.

jokes.text: Man! Now that's all I can think about! I'm gonna kill you, you no good, 56-ing...!
jokes.setup: Fry's wrong guess distracting Roberto
jokes.punchline: Roberto getting fixated on the number and using it as profanity
jokes.explanation: Great Roberto character moment - his obsessive mental instability. Using '56-ing' as a curse is creative.

jokes.text: Fifty-six? Fifty-six? Man! Now that's all I can think about!
jokes.setup: Roberto's reaction to Fry's wrong answer
jokes.punchline: Becomes obsessively fixated on the random number
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roberto mental instability - derailed by irrelevant number into obsessive loop

jokes.text: Don't kill me! I'm coming down with Stockholm Syndrome... handsome.
jokes.setup: Amy begging for her life
jokes.punchline: Immediately developing Stockholm syndrome mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Excellent psychology joke with perfect timing. The immediate shift to 'handsome' is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: I am a battle-droid, protecting the weak from crazy robots.
jokes.setup: Fry's robot identity crisis reaches climax
jokes.punchline: Discovers his 'primary function' as heroic protector
jokes.explanation: Good payoff of Fry's robot identity search - finds heroic purpose through delusion

jokes.text: No knife can penetrate my skin-tanium armor.
jokes.setup: Fry blocking Roberto's knife attack
jokes.punchline: Made-up metal combining 'skin' and 'titanium'
jokes.explanation: Decent made-up robot material. 'Skin-tanium' is a reasonable sci-fi portmanteau.

jokes.text: No, no! Don't weld me to the wall, Mommy!
jokes.setup: Roberto being defeated by Fry
jokes.punchline: Reverting to childhood trauma about being welded to wall
jokes.explanation: Dark robot childhood trauma backstory. Creative and specific to robot experience, well-integrated with Roberto's character.

jokes.text: Blood? Robots don't have blood. I must be a... A squid?
jokes.setup: Fry seeing his own blood and realizing robots don't bleed
jokes.punchline: Guessing squid instead of human
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection and character moment. Fry's stupidity shines through even in his moment of clarity. Great structure.

jokes.text: Thanks for saving us, Fry. I'm gonna continue never washing this cheek again.
jokes.setup: Amy thanking Fry for saving everyone
jokes.punchline: Reference to never washing cheek where Fry kissed her
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to running gag about Amy's hygiene regarding Fry. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: I'm gonna continue never washing this cheek again.
jokes.setup: Amy grateful to Fry for saving them
jokes.punchline: Plans to preserve his thanks kiss through poor hygiene
jokes.explanation: Mildly gross character moment showing Amy's romantic desperation

jokes.text: What?
jokes.setup: Bender saying he has the heart of a human inside him
jokes.punchline: Bender's shocked reaction to his own confession
jokes.explanation: Good ending beat showing Bender accidentally revealing something personal. His shock at his own sentiment is characteristic.
